body.title: CI grep-check enforces 'no SQLite' — narrow pattern set, soft-then-hard
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
Add a CI/pre-commit check that fails on SQLite pattern reintroduction. Narrow initial set: `import sqlite3`, `sqlite3\.`, `DatabaseProxy(`, `\.db` filename pattern (excluding approved exceptions like db_proxy.py during transition). Ships soft-warn initially (can run while T-remove-sqlite-fallback is in flight), flipped to hard-fail once removal completes. Addresses the 'rule stated but never enforced' gap that let SQLite survive for weeks.

**Affected files:** new `lab/claudecode/check_no_sqlite.py` (grep runner + pattern list). Hook into pre-commit (lab/claudecode/git_hooks/) or add to CI config — decide at sprint time based on current hook infra. Update CLAUDE.md Destructive-action blocklist to reference the check.
**Design rules:** no-sqlite: enforces the rule. No persistence — pure static check. oop-first: single check function with pattern list — functional shape appropriate, no state. docs-in-code: script top-of-file docstring names the patterns + approved-exception list + rationale. test-plan-or-why-not: applies.
**Scope boundary:** IN — grep check + CI/hook wiring + soft-warn→hard-fail flag. OUT — palace-rule-to-lint-rule general translator (separate work under T-audit-findings-to-tickets), enforcement of rules beyond no-sqlite.
**Test plan:** Unit test — feed fixture Python files with/without SQLite patterns to the checker, assert expected pass/fail. Integration — inject `import sqlite3` into a scratch file, assert check fires.
body.approved_plan: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
